queue.label: Optimize clock: memoize day/month arrays, skip re-render unless minute changes
queue.prompt: In ~/Projects/command-center/src/App.jsx, optimize the clock/date display. Two changes: (1) Move the day-name array ['Sunday','Monday',...] and month-name array ['January','February',...] outside the component as module-level constants so they're not recreated every render. (2) Split the timer: use a 60-second interval for date-only updates (which won't change for minutes at a time), and keep a 1-second interval only for the seconds display. The date display only needs to update when the minute ticks over. Keep the existing UI unchanged - this is purely a performance refactor. After changes, run `npm run build` and verify zero errors.

queue.label: Fix resetLayout: clear localStorage + reset state instead of location.reload()
queue.prompt: In ~/Projects/command-center/src/LayoutContext.jsx, fix the resetLayout() function. Currently it calls window.location.reload() which wipes all transient state (modals, unsaved edits, scroll position). Replace it with: clear the 'widgetLayout' and 'hiddenWidgets' keys from localStorage, then reset the local React state (widgets, hiddenWidgets) to default values. Do NOT call location.reload(). The existing defaults are already defined at the top of the component. After changes, run `npm run build` and verify zero errors.

queue.label: Create usePollingFetch custom hook to consolidate all fetch+poll patterns
queue.prompt: Create a new file ~/Projects/command-center/src/hooks/usePollingFetch.js with a React custom hook that consolidates the fetch + polling pattern repeated across multiple widgets. Create the directory if needed. The hook signature: usePollingFetch(url, interval, options?) where url is a string, interval is ms, and options is an optional object with: { transform: (data) => processed, retry: number (default 2), onError: (err) => void, enabled: boolean (default true), deps: array }. Returns: { data, error, isLoading, isRefreshing, lastUpdated, refetch }. Implementation details: auto-fetch on mount, re-fetch on interval, auto-cleanup on unmount, skip fetch when document.hidden is true, deduplicate in-flight requests (don't start a new fetch if previous is pending), on error keep the previous data value (don't wipe valid data). Then migrate the following files to use this hook: (1) ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/widgets/Weather.jsx - replace its manual useEffect+fetch+setInterval with usePollingFetch at 30s interval. (2) ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/widgets/Monitor.jsx - replace its manual polling with usePollingFetch. (3) ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/widgets/WorkQueue.jsx - replace its 3 separate polling useEffects with usePollingFetch. (4) ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/widgets/GatewayStatus.jsx - replace manual polling with usePollingFetch. Handle loading states consistently across all migrated widgets. After all changes, run `npm run build` and verify zero errors.

queue.label: Create centralized storage service with schema versioning and migrations
queue.prompt: Create ~/Projects/command-center/src/services/storage.js with a centralized localStorage service. Create the directory if needed. Exports: { default as storage } where storage is an object with methods: get(key, defaultValue) - reads prefixed key, JSON.parse with error handling (corrupt data logs warning + returns default), set(key, value) - JSON.stringify + write with cc_ prefix, remove(key) - remove prefixed key, clear() - remove all cc_ prefixed keys. All keys automatically prefixed with 'cc_'. Include schema versioning: read cc_schema_version on init, if version is 0 write version 1, if version doesn't match current write migration functions. Then migrate all localStorage consumers: (1) ~/Projects/command-center/src/LayoutContext.jsx - replace direct localStorage.getItem/setItem with storage service. (2) ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/widgets/Kanban.jsx - replace localStorage access with storage service. (3) ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/widgets/Todo.jsx - replace localStorage with storage service. (4) ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/widgets/Grocery.jsx - replace localStorage with storage service. (5) ~/Projects/command-center/src/ThemeContext.jsx - replace localStorage with storage service. After all changes, run `npm run build` and verify zero errors.

queue.label: Add per-widget ErrorBoundary component to prevent full dashboard crashes
queue.prompt: Create ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx - a React class component (error boundaries require class, not function). Props: name (string - widget display name), children. State: { hasError: false, error: null }. Use static getDerivedStateFromError(error) to set hasError=true. In componentDidCatch, log the error to console with the widget name. Render: if error state, show a card with the widget name, the error message, and a 'Retry' button that calls setState({ hasError: false }). If no error, render children normally. Then in ~/Projects/command-center/src/Dashboard.jsx, import ErrorBoundary and wrap each rendered widget component: <ErrorBoundary name={title}><WidgetComponent ... /></ErrorBoundary>. Make sure every widget in the grid render loop gets wrapped. After changes, run `npm run build` and verify zero errors.

queue.label: Migrate Kanban from custom drag system to @dnd-kit/core+sortable
queue.prompt: Refactor ~/Projects/command-center/src/components/widgets/Kanban.jsx to use @dnd-kit/core and @dnd-kit/sortable instead of its current 435-line custom drag system. Already installed: @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/sortable, @dnd-kit/utilities. Keep the existing data structure (cards with id, created, order, column, title, description, tags, due), keep the existing columns config, keep the existing CardEditor modal form, keep the existing localStorage persistence (already migrated to storage service). What to remove: all manual pointer-event code (onPointerDown, onPointerMove, onPointerUp), getDropTarget function, getBoundingClientRect calls, querySelector chains, activeDrag module-level state, custom z-index/transform manipulation, keyboard shortcuts (e, E, Delete, Enter). What to add: Wrap the kanban in DndContext. Each column is a useDroppable. Each card uses useSortable from dnd-kit/sortable. Use DragOverlay for a semi-transparent drag preview. On drag end: detect source and target columns, if same column use arrayMove for reorder, if different columns splice from source + add to target with position tracking. Use PointerSensor with touch support and KeyboardSensor for accessibility. The existing localStorage persistence pattern (loading on mount, saving on change) should remain but use the storage service. After all changes, run `npm run build` and verify zero errors.
